claimText: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) were adopted in 2015, comprising 17 goals and 169 targets, and succeeded the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs).
evidenceExcerpt: SDGs were adopted in 2015 (17 goals, 169 targets), succeeding the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs).
examUse: Statement-based question on the foundational facts of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

claimText: India ranked 99th in the Sustainable Development Report (SDR) 2025, marking its first time entering the top 100 in the SDG Index.
evidenceExcerpt: India ranked 99th (score 67/100) in the 10th Sustainable Development Report (SDR) 2025, entering the top 100 for the first time.
examUse: Statement-based question evaluating India's progress and current standing in global SDG indices.
distractorHooks: India has consistently been in the top 100 since 2020

claimText: The Sustainable Development Report (SDR) is released by the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN).
evidenceExcerpt: The Sustainable Development Report (SDR) is released by the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching global reports with their publishing organizations.

claimText: The Sustainable Development Goals Report (SDGR) is the only official UN report monitoring global progress and is prepared by UN DESA.
evidenceExcerpt: The Sustainable Development Goals Report (SDGR) is the only official UN report monitoring global progress, prepared by UN DESA.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing between various SDG reports and their official UN status.
distractorHooks: Prepared by the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN)

claimText: NITI Aayog is responsible for releasing the SDG India Index, the District SDG Index for NER, and India's Voluntary National Review (VNR) Report.
evidenceExcerpt: NITI Aayog releases the SDG India Index, District SDG Index for NER, and India's Voluntary National Review (VNR) Report.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nodal agency for SDG monitoring and reporting in India.
distractorHooks: Released by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI)
Released by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

claimText: The National Indicator Framework (NIF) Progress Report 2025 was launched by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI).
evidenceExcerpt: MoSPI launched the National Indicator Framework (NIF) Progress Report 2025.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching national frameworks/reports with the respective Indian ministry.
distractorHooks: Launched by the Ministry of Finance
